clues: A Swiss engineer developed this after examining plant seeds under a microscope in 1941.
It consists of two strips with hooks and loops that bond when pressed together.
Its name comes from combining the French words for velvet and hook.
This ruler's intelligence and linguistic abilities were as renowned as their appearance.
They were the last active pharaoh of ancient Egypt.
Their romantic relationships with Julius Caesar and Mark Antony are legendary.
Satoshi Tajiri created this franchise inspired by his childhood hobby of collecting insects.
Its slogan encourages you to catch them all.
Pikachu is its most recognizable character.
This dish originated as a way for Alpine villagers to use hardened cheese and stale bread during winter.
The Swiss version typically features melted cheese or chocolate.
Long forks are traditionally used to prevent double-dipping.
Competitors in this sport are called fencers and matches are called bouts.
Electronic scoring systems detect valid touches during bouts.
The three weapon types are foil, épée, and sabre.
Jean-François Champollion spent years decoding this using a trilingual stone.
The Rosetta Stone was crucial to deciphering it.
It was used extensively in ancient Egyptian tombs and temples.
This site remained unknown to the Western world until a Swiss explorer rediscovered it in 1812.
It was the capital of an ancient Nabataean kingdom.
Its Treasury building appeared in Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade.
This discovery came from observing mold contamination that created bacteria-free zones in laboratory cultures.
Alexander Fleming noticed it killed bacteria in 1928.
It became the world's first widely used antibiotic.
This mathematician was placed under house arrest for the final years of their life.
They made significant improvements to the telescope.
They discovered four moons orbiting Jupiter.
This group's original drummer was replaced before they achieved international fame.
They triggered a cultural phenomenon called mania named after them.
Their members were John, Paul, George, and Ringo.
Traditionally, field workers in Valencia cooked this over wood fires during lunch breaks.
The Valencian version traditionally includes rabbit and beans.
Saffron gives this Spanish rice dish its distinctive golden color.
British officers stationed in India brought an early version of this game back to England in the 1870s.
Players hit a feathered object over a net.
The shuttlecock can travel over 200 miles per hour during smashes.
Galileo coined the current scientific name for this phenomenon in 1619.
It appears as dancing ribbons of light in polar regions.
It's also known as the Northern Lights.
This structure took approximately 30 years to construct during the reign of King Suryavarman II.
It's the largest religious monument in the world by area.
It appears on Cambodia's national flag.
This system was adapted from a military code designed for nighttime battlefield communication.
It was invented by a French teenager who was blind.
Each character consists of up to six dots arranged in a grid.
This artist contracted polio at age six, affecting one leg permanently.
A bus accident in their youth profoundly affected their life and art.
Their distinctive unibrow became an iconic part of their image.
The working title for this show during development was 'Insomnia Cafe.'
It follows six people living in Manhattan apartments.
Central Perk is their favorite coffee shop hangout.
This dish evolved from an ancient method of preserving fish by fermenting it with rice.
Itamae refers to the specialized chefs who prepare it.
Common varieties include nigiri, maki, and sashimi.
This sport originated in medieval Scotland on frozen ponds and lochs.
Team members sweep the ice to control speed and direction.
It's sometimes called chess on ice.
Some of the stones in this monument were transported over 150 miles from Wales.
Its purpose remains debated among archaeologists.
It's located on Salisbury Plain in England.
The Bishop of Panama coined the Spanish name for this place, meaning 'saddle,' referring to tortoise shells.
It's home to species found nowhere else, including giant tortoises.
Charles Darwin visited these Ecuadorian islands in 1835.
This device replaced vacuum tubes and made electronics exponentially smaller and more efficient.
Its invention at Bell Labs earned three scientists the Nobel Prize.
Modern computer chips contain billions of these miniaturized components.
This activist had been trained in civil disobedience techniques at the Highlander Folk School.
They refused to give up their seat on a Montgomery bus in 1955.
Their courage helped catalyze the American civil rights movement.
J.K. Rowling conceived the main character during a delayed train journey from Manchester to London.
The protagonist attends a school for witchcraft and wizardry.
The main antagonist is a dark wizard named Voldemort.
Legend claims this pastry was created to celebrate a military victory over Ottoman forces.
Its dough is folded repeatedly with butter to create flaky layers.
Its crescent shape is iconic in French bakeries.
Persian manuscripts from 600 BCE contain the earliest known references to this sport.
Players use mallets while mounted on animals.
It's often called the sport of kings.
Hermann analyzed how patients perceived standardized ambiguous stimuli to understand their personality structure.
Interpretation of these inkblots supposedly reveals personality traits.
It was developed by a Swiss psychiatrist in 1921.
Some archaeologists believe this location inspired Plato's legend of Atlantis.
A massive eruption around 1600 BCE shaped its current crescent form.
It's one of Greece's most photographed destinations.
This system became fully operational in 1995 after decades of development and satellite launches.
It was initially developed for military applications.
Your smartphone uses it to pinpoint your location anywhere on Earth.
This leader was imprisoned on Robben Island alongside other political activists.
They became their country's first Black president in 1994.
They won the Nobel Peace Prize for their work toward reconciliation.
George Lucas created this space opera franchise.
It features a mystical power called 'The Force'.
The first film was released in 1977.
Originating in Naples, Italy, this dish is popular worldwide.
It typically consists of a round, flattened dough topped with tomatoes, cheese, and various other ingredients.
Margherita is a classic variety with basil, mozzarella, and tomato.
Invented by James Naismith in 1891.
Players aim to shoot a ball through a hoop elevated 10 feet high.
It is played by two teams of five players on a rectangular court.
